key_insight: Adding SageMath to a ReAct-style LLM agent substantially improves research-level math problem solving, especially for computational and experimental mathematics.
engineering_relevance: This suggests CAS-augmented LLM agents can better support mathematicians by combining reasoning, verification, and up-to-date documentation in a practical research loop.
method: ReAct agent combines LLM reasoning with SageMath for verifiable computation.
Context7 provides current documentation during problem solving.
RealMath benchmark is refined with multi-step post-processing and multi-stage validation.
evidence_signal: Across frontier models on RealMath, SageMath access improves performance by 9.7 percentage points on average, with gains ranging from 1.5 to 27.8 points; GPT-5.5 reaches 75.2% solve rate and the lowest token usage among tool-enabled setups.
watch_item: Results depend on benchmark extraction and validation quality, so the reported gains may not fully generalize to all mathematical tasks or tool workflows.
paper_title: Evaluating SageMath-Augmented LLM Agents for Computational and Experimental Mathematics
